text:
{
  "english": {
    "IsA": [
      "It is a board wargame.",
      "It is a simulation of the conflict between Germany and the Soviet Union during World War II."
    ],
    "HasA": [
      "It has wooden blocks instead of the traditional die-cut cardboard counters.",
      "It has a 16.5\" x 22\" paper hex grid map.",
      "It has 132 wooden blocks and 22 wooden markers.",
      "It has a 12-page rule book.",
      "It has four scenario cards.",
      "It has four 10-sided die.",
      "It has eight scenarios.",
      "It has a short introductory scenario.",
      "It has a \"production phase\".",
      "It has \"I go, You go\" player turns."
    ],
    "HasFeatureOf": [
      "It is characterized by the use of wooden blocks.",
      "It is characterized by limited knowledge about the opposing forces.",
      "It is characterized by strength ratings displayed on the unit's face.",
      "It is characterized by step reductions.",
      "It is characterized by scenarios that can be played separately or linked.",
      "It is characterized by \"Blitz\" speed movement.",
      "It is characterized by combat resolution based on dice rolls.",
      "It is characterized by strength reduction by turning the wooden block.",
      "It is characterized by a computer adaptation.",
      "It is characterized by new cover art in its second edition."
    ],
    "UsedFor": [
      "It is used for simulating the conflict between Germany and the Soviet Union during World War II.",
      "It is used for engaging opposing forces.",
      "It is used for displaying unit strength ratings.",
      "It is used for covering approximately six months of conflict.",
      "It is used for forming a single campaign game.",
      "It is used for acquainting players with the rules.",
      "It is used for activating all Headquarter units.",
      "It is used for moving units.",
      "It is used for scoring hits in combat.",
      "It is used for combining with WestFront into one massive wargame."
    ],

    "Affects": [
      "It affects the opposing player's knowledge of forces.",
      "It affects the strength of the defending unit.",
      "It affects combat effectiveness when using \"Blitz\" speed."
    ],
    "ConsistsOf": [
      "It consists of a paper hex grid map.",
      "It consists of wooden blocks and wooden markers.",
      "It consists of a rule book.",
      "It consists of scenario cards.",
      "It consists of 10-sided die.",
      "It consists of eight scenarios.",
      "It consists of \"production phase\" and player turns."
    ],
    "PartOf": [
      "It is part of a massive wargame covering the entire European theatre when combined with WestFront."
    ],
    "MemberOf": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "BelongsTo": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "KnownFor": [
      "It is known for its use of wooden blocks.",
      "It is known for simulating the Eastern Front of World War II.",
      "It is known for its \"fog of war\" effect.",
      "It is known for being a recommended buy for anyone interested in the subject.",
      "It is known for being one of the best Russian campaign games.",
      "It is known for winning several awards in 1991."
    ],
    "AssociatedWith": [
      "It is associated with Columbia Games.",
      "It is associated with Tom Dalgliesh.",
      "It is associated with Craig Besinque.",
      "It is associated with Eric Hotz.",
      "It is associated with Operation Barbarossa.",
      "It is associated with the conflict between Germany and the Soviet Union during World War II.",
      "It is associated with Microsoft Windows.",
      "It is associated with WestFront.",
      "It is associated with EuroFront.",
      "It is associated with David Pentland."
    ],
    "NamedAfter": [
      "It is named after the Eastern Front of World War II."
    ],
    "OccurredIn": [
      "It occurred in 1991.",
      "It occurred in 1974.",
      "It occurred in 1992.",
      "It occurred in 1995.",
      "It occurred in 2006."
    ],
    "HeldIn": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "WonBy": [
      "It was won by the Origins Award for \"Best Modern-Day Boardgame of 1991\".",
      "It was won by the James F. Dunnigan Award for \"Best Playability & Design of 1991\".",
      "It was won by the Charles S. Roberts Award for \"Best World War II Board Game of 1991\"."
    ],
    "About": [
      "It is about the conflict between Germany and the Soviet Union during World War II.",
      "It is about Operation Barbarossa.",
      "It is about the Russian drive on Berlin.",
      "It is about \"Operation Edelweiss\".",
      "It is about the Eastern Front of World War II."
    ],
    "InfluencedBy": [
      "It was influenced by Napoléon: The Waterloo Campaign, 1815.",
      "It was influenced by the favorable reaction to block games.",
      "It was influenced by the desire to produce \"block games\"."

      "It influenced the release of Computer EastFront.",
      "It influenced the release of WestFront.",
      "It influenced the release of EuroFront.",
      "It influenced the release of a second edition of EastFront, WestFront, and EuroFront."

  "japanese": {
    "IsA": [
      "それはボードウォーゲームです。",
      "それは第二次世界大戦中のドイツとソビエト連邦の紛争のシミュレーションです。"
    ],
    "HasA": [
      "それは伝統的なダイカットボール紙カウンターの代わりに木製ブロックを使用しています。",
      "それは16.5インチx22インチの紙製ヘックスグリッドマップを持っています。",
      "それは132個の木製ブロックと22個の木製マーカーを持っています。",
      "それは12ページのルールブックを持っています。",
      "それは4枚のシナリオカードを持っています。",
      "それは4つの10面ダイを持っています。",
      "それは8つのシナリオを持っています。",
      "それは短い導入シナリオを持っています。",
      "それは「生産フェーズ」を持っています。",
      "それは「私が先、あなたが先」のプレイヤーターンを持っています。"
    ],
    "HasFeatureOf": [
      "それは木製ブロックの使用を特徴としています。",
      "それは敵軍に関する限られた知識を特徴としています。",
      "それはユニットの前面に表示される強さの評価を特徴としています。",
      "それはステップ削減を特徴としています。",
      "それは個別に、またはキャンペーンゲームとしてリンクしてプレイできるシナリオを特徴としています。",
      "それは「ブリッツ」スピード移動を特徴としています。",
      "それはサイコロの目で解決される戦闘を特徴としています。",
      "それは木製ブロックを回転させることによる強さの削減を特徴としています。",
      "それはコンピュータアダプテーションを特徴としています。",
      "それは第2版で新しいカバーアートを特徴としています。"
    ],
    "UsedFor": [
      "それは第二次世界大戦中のドイツとソビエト連邦の紛争のシミュレーションに使用されます。",
      "それは敵軍との交戦に使用されます。",
      "それはユニットの強さの評価を表示するために使用されます。",
      "それは約6ヶ月間の紛争をカバーするために使用されます。",
      "それは単一のキャンペーンゲームを形成するために使用されます。",
      "それはルールに慣れるために使用されます。",
      "それはすべての司令部ユニットを起動するために使用されます。",
      "それはユニットの移動に使用されます。",
      "それは戦闘でヒットを記録するために使用されます。",
      "それは西フロントと組み合わせて、ヨーロッパ戦域全体をカバーする大規模なウォーゲームの一部として使用されます。"
    ],
    "LocatedIn": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "BuiltIn": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "BuiltBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "OriginatedIn": [
      "それは第二次世界大戦中のドイツとソビエト連邦の紛争に由来します。"
    ],
    "FoundedBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "FoundedIn": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "CreatedBy": [
      "それはトム・ダルグリーシュとクレイグ・ベシンクによって作成されました。"
    ],
    "CreatedIn": [
      "それは1991年に作成されました。"
    ],
    "DevelopedBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "DevelopedIn": [

    ],

    "DiscoveredIn": [

    ],
    "InventedIn": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "PublishedIn": [
      "それは1991年に出版されました。",
      "それは2006年に出版されました。"
    ],
    "WrittenBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "ComposedBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "DirectedBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "PerformedBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],

      "unknown"
    ],
    "ProducedBy": [
      "それはコロンビア・ゲームズによって制作されました。"
    ],
    "CausedBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "Affects": [
      "それは敵プレイヤーの部隊に関する知識に影響を与えます。",
      "それは防御ユニットの強さに影響を与えます。",
      "それは「ブリッツ」スピードを使用する際の戦闘効率に影響を与えます。"
    ],
    "ConsistsOf": [
      "それは紙製ヘックスグリッドマップで構成されています。",
      "それは木製ブロックと木製マーカーで構成されています。",
      "それはルールブックで構成されています。",
      "それはシナリオカードで構成されています。",
      "それは10面ダイで構成されています。",
      "それは8つのシナリオで構成されています。",
      "それは「生産フェーズ」とプレイヤーターンで構成されています。"
    ],
    "PartOf": [
      "それは西フロントと組み合わされたときに、ヨーロッパ戦域全体をカバーする大規模なウォーゲームの一部です。"
    ],
    "MemberOf": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "BelongsTo": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "KnownFor": [
      "それは木製ブロックの使用で知られています。",
      "それは第二次世界大戦の東部戦線をシミュレートすることで知られています。",
      "それは「戦争の霧」効果で知られています。",
      "それは対象に関心のある人にとって推奨される購入として知られています。",
      "それは最高のロシア戦線ゲームの一つとして知られています。",
      "それは1991年にいくつかの賞を受賞したことで知られています。"
    ],
    "AssociatedWith": [
      "それはコロンビア・ゲームズと関連があります。",
      "それはトム・ダルグリーシュと関連があります。",
      "それはクレイグ・ベシンクと関連があります。",
      "それはエリック・ホッツと関連があります。",
      "それはバルバロッサ作戦と関連があります。",
      "それは第二次世界大戦中のドイツとソビエト連邦の紛争と関連があります。",
      "それはマイクロソフトウィンドウズと関連があります。",
      "それは西フロントと関連があります。",
      "それはユーロフロントと関連があります。",
      "それはデイビッド・ペントランドと関連があります。"
    ],
    "NamedAfter": [
      "それは第二次世界大戦の東部戦線にちなんで名付けられました。"
    ],
    "OccurredIn": [
      "それは1991年に発生しました。",
      "それは1974年に発生しました。",
      "それは1992年に発生しました。",
      "それは1995年に発生しました。",
      "それは2006年に発生しました。"
    ],
    "HeldIn": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "WonBy": [
      "それは1991年の「最優秀現代ボードゲーム」オリジンズ賞を受賞しました。",
      "それは1991年の「最優秀プレイアビリティ＆デザイン」ジェームズ・F・ダニガン賞を受賞しました。",
      "それは1991年の「最優秀第二次世界大戦ボードゲーム」チャールズ・S・ロバーツ賞を受賞しました。"
    ],
    "About": [
      "それは第二次世界大戦中のドイツとソビエト連邦の紛争についてです。",
      "それはバルバロッサ作戦についてです。",
      "それはベルリンへのロシアの進撃についてです。",
      "それは「エーデルワイス作戦」についてです。",
      "それは第二次世界大戦の東部戦線についてです。"
    ],
    "InfluencedBy": [
      "それはナポレオン：ワーテルローの戦い、1815年に影響を受けました。",
      "それはブロックゲームに対する好意的な反応に影響を受けました。",
      "それは「ブロックゲーム」を制作したいという願望に影響を受けました。"
    ],
    "Influenced": [
      "それはコンピュータ・イーストフロントのリリースに影響を与えました。",
      "それはウエストフロントのリリースに影響を与えました。",
      "それはユーロフロントのリリースに影響を与えました。",
      "それはイーストフロント、ウエストフロント、ユーロフロントの第2版のリリースに影響を与えました。"
    ]
  }
}

IsA: It is a simulation of the conflict between Germany and the Soviet Union during World War II.
HasA: It has wooden blocks instead of the traditional die-cut cardboard counters.
It has a 16.5" x 22" paper hex grid map.
It has 132 wooden blocks and 22 wooden markers.
It has a 12-page rule book.
It has four scenario cards.
It has four 10-sided die.
It has eight scenarios.
It has a short introductory scenario.
It has a "production phase".
It has "I go, You go" player turns.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by the use of wooden blocks.
It is characterized by limited knowledge about the opposing forces.
It is characterized by strength ratings displayed on the unit's face.
It is characterized by step reductions.
It is characterized by scenarios that can be played separately or linked.
It is characterized by "Blitz" speed movement.
It is characterized by combat resolution based on dice rolls.
It is characterized by strength reduction by turning the wooden block.
It is characterized by a computer adaptation.
It is characterized by new cover art in its second edition.
UsedFor: It is used for simulating the conflict between Germany and the Soviet Union during World War II.
It is used for engaging opposing forces.
It is used for displaying unit strength ratings.
It is used for covering approximately six months of conflict.
It is used for forming a single campaign game.
It is used for acquainting players with the rules.
It is used for activating all Headquarter units.
It is used for moving units.
It is used for scoring hits in combat.
It is used for combining with WestFront into one massive wargame.
OriginatedIn: It originated in the conflict between Germany and the Soviet Union during World War II.
CreatedBy: It was created by Tom Dalgliesh and Craig Besinque.
ProducedBy: It was produced by Columbia Games.
Affects: It affects the opposing player's knowledge of forces.
It affects the strength of the defending unit.
It affects combat effectiveness when using "Blitz" speed.
ConsistsOf: It consists of a paper hex grid map.
It consists of wooden blocks and wooden markers.
It consists of a rule book.
It consists of "production phase" and player turns.
PartOf: It is part of a massive wargame covering the entire European theatre when combined with WestFront.
KnownFor: It is known for its use of wooden blocks.
It is known for simulating the Eastern Front of World War II.
It is known for its "fog of war" effect.
It is known for being a recommended buy for anyone interested in the subject.
It is known for being one of the best Russian campaign games.
It is known for winning several awards in 1991.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with Columbia Games.
It is associated with Tom Dalgliesh.
It is associated with Craig Besinque.
It is associated with Eric Hotz.
It is associated with Operation Barbarossa.
It is associated with the conflict between Germany and the Soviet Union during World War II.
It is associated with Microsoft Windows.
It is associated with WestFront.
It is associated with EuroFront.
It is associated with David Pentland.
NamedAfter: It is named after the Eastern Front of World War II.
WonBy: It was won by the Origins Award for "Best Modern-Day Boardgame of 1991".
It was won by the James F. Dunnigan Award for "Best Playability & Design of 1991".
It was won by the Charles S. Roberts Award for "Best World War II Board Game of 1991".
About: It is about the conflict between Germany and the Soviet Union during World War II.
It is about the Russian drive on Berlin.
It is about the Eastern Front of World War II.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by Napoléon: The Waterloo Campaign, 1815.
It was influenced by the favorable reaction to block games.
It was influenced by the desire to produce "block games".
Influenced: It influenced the release of Computer EastFront.
It influenced the release of WestFront.
It influenced the release of EuroFront.
It influenced the release of a second edition of EastFront, WestFront, and EuroFront.
